A grid patterned table holds a light blue rectangular towel with a black and white sheep design on its right side. A human hand extends from the left above the towel's left edge. The hand is open and ready to grasp. The task is to pick up the left edge of the towel using the thumb and the index fingers and fold it right to align with the right edge. The hand grasps the left edge using the thumb and the index fingers and lifts it. The hand folds the towel over to the right edge. The hand aligns the edges and presses down to secure the fold. The towel appears soft. The hand releases the towl and lifts up to stay still in the air. The towel now occupies half its original width on the table. The entire human hand must remain fully visible in the frame at all times, with no cropping, no fingers cut off, and no part of the hand outside the camera view. The camera must remain completely static with no movement, no panning, no tilting, no zooming, and no change in viewpoint during the entire video. No extra or unnecessary motions are allowed, the hand must not perform any additional gestures such as turning to show the palm, posing, rotating unnecessarily. All other objects in the scene that are not being manipulated must remain completely stationary, with no position shift, no rotation, and no change in placement throughout the entire video.
